characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Disproportionate Retribution:Maya and her friends humiliate her in front of the school so she gets even by murdering all of them. Evil Redhead: She's got red hair and is a budding serial killer. Face–Heel Turn:In Part One, she is just a normal teenage girl, and seemed like a decent enough person, but then she murders the girls who bullied her and, by Part Two, she is willing to murder innocent people who didn't do anything to her. Moral Sociopathy: She's got a very loose moral compass and a major sadistic streak but is capable of genuine love for others and will generally avoid going after anyone who hasn't done anything wrong. Pint-Sized Powerhouse: She is only about five feet tall but is able to take down much bigger opponents, usually by fighting dirty.

characters.name: Scarlett Winslow
characters.description: Disproportionate Retribution:Maya and her friends humiliate her in front of the school so she gets even by murdering all of them. Evil Redhead: She's got red hair and is a budding serial killer. Face–Heel Turn:In Part One, she is just a normal teenage girl, and seemed like a decent enough person, but then she murders the girls who bullied her and, by Part Two, she is willing to murder innocent people who didn't do anything to her. Moral Sociopathy: She's got a very loose moral compass and a major sadistic streak but is capable of genuine love for others and will generally avoid going after anyone who hasn't done anything wrong. Pint-Sized Powerhouse: She is only about five feet tall but is able to take down much bigger opponents, usually by fighting dirty.

characters.name: Maya
characters.description: Bitch in Sheep's Clothing:She seems very nice but only invited Scarlett to her house to humiliate her. The Bully:She manipulated Scarlett into trusting her to confide in her about her BDSM fetish, and was livestreaming the whole thing so her classmates would see it. Kick the Dog:She humiliates Scarlett in front of the school just for fun. Manipulative Bastard:She gets Scarlett to trust her just to ruin her reputation. Pet the Dog:She eventually eases up towards Scarlett and abandons her plans for revenge, allowing her to say goodbye to her dads and leave the house.

characters.name: RubyMcDaniel
characters.description: Abusive Parents: Her parents faked their death and sold her to an uncle who tortured her for fun and is all but stated to have been sexually abusive and later revealed they weren't actually her parents, just people who kidnapped and sold her. Even Evil Has Loved Ones: As sadistic as she is, she genuinely loves Scarlett and grows to care similarly about Troy and Michael. Even Evil Has Standards: As much as she may not like her, she refuses to let Maya and her friends hurt Shanti out of loyalty to Scarlett. Freudian Excuse: She was kidnapped at a young age and sold between people who used her for sadistic torture and abuse, leaving her with an extreme anger towards the world. Sadist: She gets pleasure out of making people suffer, including innocent people.

characters.name: Dr. Andi Grant
characters.description: The Determinator: Evenin death, she won't stop doing her job.

characters.name: Adam
characters.description: Asshole Victim:He tries to blackmail Michael and Troy into having sex with him, and murders Martin, so he really had it coming when Ruby stabbed him.

characters.name: Tony Peterson
characters.description: Asshole Victim:It is pretty satisfying when Scarlett murders him. Hate Sink: He's the only person in the story to have no redeeming or sympathetic traits whatsoever. Sadist: He relentlessly tortured poor Ruby, physically and emotionally, just for enjoyment.

characters.name: Kelley
characters.description: Uncertain Doom:Rabbit Rabbit is displayed as a "Trending New Title" on her computer while she and Chad aren't paying attention, and what happens after isn't shown before the episode ends.

characters.name: Chad
characters.description: Uncertain Doom:Rabbit Rabbit is displayed as a "Trending New Title" on Kelley's computer while he and Kelley aren't paying attention, and what happens after isn't shown before the episode ends.

characters.name: Rabid Ruth
characters.description: Cassandra Truth:She was right about the movie Rabbit Rabbit causing people to become violent and kill each other.

characters.name: Larry Bitterman
characters.description: Asshole Victim:He gets kneecapped by Chad and Kelley and is left to burn to death in his trailer. The Bad Guy Wins:He doesn't live to see it, butRabbit Rabbitends up being broadcast for the entire public on Netflix, just like he intended. Big Bad: Of "Drive In", being the creator ofRabbit Rabbit. Bloodbath Villain Origin: His backstory is described as him being put on trial after his movie caused the audience to go insane and tear each other apart. Brown Note: His movie was created with audio and visual stimuli that drive people insane.

characters.name: Zinn
characters.description: Asshole Victim: A huge douchebag who openly introduces himself as bad news, and very evidently proves it in the span of just one episode. He cross lines such as filming and mocking a man who ends his own life and does nothing to help him, sexually harasses an innocent woman working as a mall elf, and tells a line full of children that Santa doesn't exist. All for content for his dyingYouTubechannel. He get's the worst death of the boys in the Bro House, and asBad Santapromised him, got exactly what he deserved for Christmas. Cruel and Unusual Death: Is beaten by theBad Santawith a giant stick to incapacitate him, thereby he gets mistletoe shoved in his mouth and wrapped in Christmas lights as hepathetically apologizesto theBad Santafor mercy in vain. Then he's thrown into the pool andslowly electrocuted. Frat Bro: Behaves exactly like a college frat boy despite actually just being a 26 year oldManchild. His friends run aYouTubechannel where they all behave exactly like douchey college boys despite all being in their late 20's. Instant Humiliation: Just Add YouTube!: Basically his entire motivation in the episode. Whether it be dumping ice on Barry while he's in the hot tub, filming a man end his own life, sexually harassing a woman, bullying a little person, antagonizing theBad Santaantagonist who turns out to be a serial killer, or telling a line full of children that Santa doen't exist. Zinn's actions to film shocking content and humiliate others come from a desperate desire to hit 5 million subscribers and he has no problem humiliating and hurting others to do so. Jerkass: The worst one of the Bro House even though they're all already awful. Every scene of him displays what a prick he is, sexually harassment, heightism towards a little person, hazing, tellingyoung children there's no such thing as Santa, and obviously what kicks off the plot is him leading the charge in filming a man jump to his death from a bridge and discourage Barry from calling the police to

characters.name: Barry
characters.description: Token Good Teammate: He's the only member of the Bro House who isn't a complete asshole. Not that it spares him from being killed at the end.

characters.name: James
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: James gets excited at the idea of creating homoerotic content for the benefit of Bro House's female fans, and ends up getting a poorly concealed erection when Zinn and Wyatt are licking ice cream off his stomach for one of said videos. He eventually admits that he did get an erection, but it's still left unclear whether he's actually into men. Black Dude Dies First: Subverted - he's the second to last to meet his demise.

characters.name: Wyatt
characters.description: Jerkass: He gets excited when he sees a man about to commit suicide, because he believes theirYouTubechannel will get more publicity. He doesn't try to help in any way, or even feel sympathy for the man, he just keeps filming.

characters.name: "Santa"
characters.description: Bad Santa: Not the actual Santa, but he fits as he's aSerial Killerdressed as Santa. Revenge:He kills the Bro House guys because they made fun of him.

characters.name: Liv Whitley
characters.description: Batman Gambit:Once she finds out that Ba'al isn't real (at least not the one she saw), and that Matt put together a whole scheme to get her out of his life, she summoned the real Ba'al and had him kill Matt's friends and have Matt framed for it, and let him rot in prison. The Dog Bites Back: Afterbeing gaslit over the course of several weeks by her husband and his friends as part of a scheme to get her inheritance, she cooks together a scheme of her own to get revenge on them all. She lampshades this in her final conversation with Matt, pointing out how ridiculous it is thathe's asking for her help after everything he did to her. Mama Bear: She'll face down a terrifying demon to protect her son. Whenshe finds out Matt's scheme and his plans to essentially neglect their son to the point of abandonment, shewreckshis life.

characters.name: Matt Webb
characters.description: Asshole Victim:He set up a whole elaborate scheme to make his wife believe that a demon was trying to take their baby son's soul, all so she would accidentally injure him and get sent to a mental hospital and out of his life. He ends up in prison after being wrongfully convicted of murdering his friends, and Liv lets him rot in prison. Gold Digger: Subverted - he makes it clear to Liv that he loves her for who she is and doesn't want to depend on just her family's money to get by. Then itsDouble Subvertedwhenit turns out he and his friends all gaslit her over the course of several months to get her committed to a mental asylum to take her whole fortune. Laser-Guided Karma:Matt pulled off an elaborate scheme to make Liv think she was crazy and be locked up for her claims of being haunted by a demon. He ends up locked in prison for life. Pet the Dog:The one moment he shows he has a conscience is when he warns Liv not to go to their house because there's an actual demon there, although, unbeknownst to Matt, she is actually working with him.

characters.name: Bernadette
characters.description: Bitch in Sheep's Clothing:She initially appears to be helpful, sweet, and sympathetic to Liv's struggle to conceive but it was actually all a ruse for her part in Matt's scheme. Failed a Spot Check: It never occurred to Bernadette to actually read the ritual book she gave to Liv or consider why the shop she bought it from seemed eager for her to have it. Neck Snap: How she is killed by Ba'al. Compared to the others, she got off lucky.

characters.name: Emma
characters.description: Asshole Victim:She carved the figurines as part of the scheme to gaslight Liv. She ends up being brutally killed later on. Laser-Guided Karma:She meets her end at the hands of the actual demonic entity she and her friends used in their scheme to gaslight Liv.

characters.name: Rory
characters.description: Asshole Victim:He wore the demon suit as part of the scheme to gaslight Liv. He ends up being brutally killed later on. Laser-Guided Karma:He meets his end at the hands of the actual demonic entity he and his friends used in their scheme to gaslight Liv.

characters.name: Stan
characters.description: Asshole Victim:He was the one to add the special effects to the baby monitor, the damage to the basement, and electronics to supply the devilish audio as part of the scheme to gaslight Liv. He ends up being brutally killed later on. Laser-Guided Karma:He meets his end at the hands of the actual demonic entity he and his friends used in their scheme to gaslight Liv.

characters.name: Norma
characters.description: Ethnic Menial Labor: She's a Latina housekeeper working for a wealthy Beverly Hills socialite. Screw This, I'm Outta Here!: When she sees the demonic Ba'al totem figurine, she draws a cross over herself and immediately runs out of the house.

characters.name: Ba'al
characters.description: Big Red Devil: When not covered in shadow, he looks like a tall, imposing red demon. Mystical Pregnancy: Can give fertility to women, allowing Liv to have a child.Except that was the result of Matt allowing himself to release sperm at that time. The real one Liv summons can do this, although he does it in a...differentway. Real After All: No one seems to believe Liv when Ba'al starts to target her child.Untilit was revealed that Matt and his friends gaslit Liv to get her out of his life.Thenshe summons theactualBa'al, using the "fake" ritual made by Bernadette. Villain Baal: Downplayed - he's a big scary demonic entity, but is completely bound to the will of those who summon him.
